{"action":"closed","number":46,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/ceres-solver-feedstock/pulls/46","id":2177891431,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCue2L86Bz_xn","html_url":"https://github.com/conda-forge/ceres-solver-feedstock/pull/46","diff_url":"https://github.com/conda-forge/ceres-solver-feedstock/pull/46.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/conda-forge/ceres-solver-feedstock/pull/46.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/ceres-solver-feedstock/issues/46","number":46,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Simplify FindSuiteSparse, dependencies","user":{"login":"minrk","id":151929,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE1MTkyOQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/151929?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk","html_url":"https://github.com/minrk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/minrk/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"upstream's FindSuiteSparse is very complex and needs to handle a lot of situations. It also searches for all of _suitesparse's_ dependencies, which is the wrong thing to do when suitesparse is a shared library, leading to problems like https://github.com/conda-forge/suitesparse-feedstock/pull/97

I replaced it with a far simpler FindSuiteSparse which relies on SuiteSparse's own cmake config files, which we can safely assume here in conda-forge, while upstream cannot. I also included a test for `find_package(Ceres)` to make sure this all works, which didn't before.

Several dependencies in this recipe appear to have been present due to ceres searching for transitive dependencies that are not required and not direct dependencies of ceres:

needed for upstream FindSuiteSparse to pass, not actually used:
- libblas, cblas (libblas needed for cmake's FindLAPACK on linux and Windows, but not linked)
- tbb

transitive dependency of glog, not used directly:
- gflags

Also some minor cleanup:

- removed run dependencies for packages that have `run_exports`
- added version pinning to the eigen `run` requirement, matching what is in `run_exports` (closes #19)
- add missing host dependency on `metis`, which Ceres _does_ link directly when using EIGENMETIS (it also links it directly when suitesparse does, which is not correct)
- remove some redundant/unused cmake arguments already set in $CMAKE_ARGS

{"repository_id":182957615,"push_id":21507734752,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"81e0a73a1cb8e9e0d66d52734f2514970a1ca20c","before":"ddd101d181e170a715e44d0439450a70543f1d35","commits":[{"sha":"81e0a73a1cb8e9e0d66d52734f2514970a1ca20c","author":{"email":"408640eb0c100f875dbe3f1b67bd9a73ee0274f2@gmail.com","name":"Min RK"},"message":"Simplify FindSuiteSparse, dependencies (#46)

* simplify FindSuiteSparse

upstream FindSuiteSparse is overly complex and looks for a lot of things it doesn't need

- remove unneeded run dependencies (run_exports)
- remove unused dependencies on blas, tbb, only needed for FindSuiteSparse

* MNT: Re-rendered with conda-build 24.9.0, conda-smithy 3.44.3, and conda-forge-pinning 2024.11.12.14.24.54

* test helloworld example with cmake

* put libblas back

not needed on mac, but needed elsewhere

* MNT: Re-rendered with conda-build 24.9.0, conda-smithy 3.44.3, and conda-forge-pinning 2024.11.12.14.24.54

* skip cross-compiled tests

* libblas isn't linked, even though it's needed to find lapack

* FindSuiteSparse doesn't need metis

* maybe emulated test build just needed make","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/ceres-solver-feedstock/commits/81e0a73a1cb8e9e0d66d52734f2514970a1ca20c"}]}

also generalize the existing EMBED_RESOURCE_FILES option to embed the ITRFxxxx resource files.

```
.. option:: EMBED_GRIDS_DIRECTORY=<directory>

    .. versionadded:: 9.6

    Embed files from <directory> ending with .tif or .json in the PROJ library itself.

    The pointed directory can potentially be the full PROJ-data package (uncompressed).
    In that case, about 6 GB of free disk and 16 GB of RAM are required to build PROJ.

    When using this parameter, EMBED_RESOURCE_FILES must be set to ON.

    If the content of the directory changes, you need to run CMake again to
    update the list of files.
```

Solution to issue cannot be found in the documentation.

- [x] I checked the documentation.

### Issue

draco is built without `draco_transcoder` which is part of `draco` since version 1.5.0.

Looks like it needs to be build with specific flags: https://github.com/google/draco/blob/main/BUILDING.md#transcoder

Is it possible to provide a build that includes the `draco_transcoder` binary?

Here are my hopes-and-dreams for a GDAL CLI refactor effort:

1. A commitment to full compatibility of old-school CLIs with a clear deprecation and removal schedule. Some lightly-used CLI utilities could probably be folded into `gdal` in a release or two, but others like `gdalinfo`, `ogrinfo`, `gdalwarp`, and `gdal_translate` are probably never to be deprecated, let alone removed. 
2. A [`pdal pipeline`](https://pdal.io/en/latest/pipeline.html)-style yaml/json syntax that can be read via `--stdin` from the `gdal` command that cleanly maps to any and all options, configurations, and settings the CLI provides. This solves  @tbonfort's complaint about parsing by giving programmatic CLI users the ability to script, validate, and execute their workflows in the language of their choice. Even better would be a schema that could be validated.
3. Consistent specification of options, relationships, arguments, and inputs
4. VRT awareness
5. A commitment to clean up various mini-languages (data source definitions being a particularly arbitrary one)

@sgillies post at https://sgillies.net/2024/11/09/a-new-cli-for-gdal.html for more background about boulders we tolerate stepping over is vary apt. These disrupt our ability to "think" in GDAL CLI, and any refactor effort should identify the tallest ones and focus on them first.

### Release Notes

### [`v7.7.7`](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/compare/2.8.1...7.7.7)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/compare/2.8.1...7.7.7)

### [`v2.8.1`](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/releases/tag/2.8.1)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/compare/2.8.0...2.8.1)

##### Bug Fixes

-   Include `<cstdlib>` for gcc-15 (thanks [@​t0b3](https://redirect.github.com/t0b3)!) [#​4537](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4537)
-   Don't validate LAS header in `readers.las` until it is complete [#​4534](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4534)
-   Add `cls` and `class` as `alt_name` for `Classification` [#​4532](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4532)
-   Fetch bounds in `readers.ply` `preview()` [#​4529](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4529)
-   `readers.copc` might have memory leak in some situations (thanks [@​AbhinavAtrishi](https://redirect.github.com/AbhinavAtrishi)!) [#​4511](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4511)
-   Consolidate `PDAL_VENDOR_EIGEN_DIR` CMake variable definition [#​4503](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4503)
-   Allow override/set of `--breakout` dimension for `pdal info` [#​4502](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4502)
-   Use `processOne` instead of custom code in standard mode of `writers.text` [#​4501](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4501)
-   Always add Classification flag dimensions for laszip-based EPT data in `readers.ept` (thanks [@​connormanning](https://redirect.github.com/connormanning)!) [#​4500](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4500)

##### Documentation

-   Documentation migrated to Jupyterbook (thanks [@​chambbj](https://redirect.github.com/chambbj)!) [#​4495](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4495) [#​4527](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4527)
-   Document how to write additional attributes (thanks [@​oleg-alexandrov](https://redirect.github.com/oleg-alexandrov)!) [#​4522](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4522)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/compare/2.8.0...2.8.1

### [`v2.8.0`](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/releases/tag/2.8.0)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/compare/2.7.2...2.8.0)

##### Changes of Note

-   Warn instead of error for when global encoding is not set by [@​hobu](https://redirect.github.com/hobu) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4414](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4414)
-   Default writers.las to LAS 1.4 by [@​hobu](https://redirect.github.com/hobu) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4482](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4482)
-   Default writers.las.dataformat_id to 7 by [@​hobu](https://redirect.github.com/hobu) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4485](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4485)

##### New Stages

-   Implement `filters.label_duplicates` by [@​hobu](https://redirect.github.com/hobu) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4469](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4469)
-   Add `filters.expressionstats` and `pdal info --breakout` option by [@​hobu](https://redirect.github.com/hobu) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4487](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4487)

##### Enhancements

-   TileDB option to enable/disable duplicate points by [@​normanb](https://redirect.github.com/normanb) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4370](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4370)
-   implement 'auto' as default CRS for BPF writer [#​4418](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4418) by [@​hobu](https://redirect.github.com/hobu) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4428](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4428)
-   Allow forcing VLR creation as EVLR  by [@​hobu](https://redirect.github.com/hobu) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4443](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4443)
-   add 'bins' item to filters.stats output by [@​hobu](https://redirect.github.com/hobu) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4477](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4477)
-   Optional stable sort for filters.sort by [@​abellgithub](https://redirect.github.com/abellgithub) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4478](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4478)
-   Support sorting multiple dimensions at once in `filters.sort` by [@​hobu](https://redirect.github.com/hobu) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4468](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4468)

##### Bug Fixes

-   Don't break strict aliasing rules. by [@​abellgithub](https://redirect.github.com/abellgithub) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4361](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4361)
-   Don't do LAS ready processing if there are no points. by [@​abellgithub](https://redirect.github.com/abellgithub) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4363](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4363)
-   Make sure all stages are initialized before prepared is called. by [@​abellgithub](https://redirect.github.com/abellgithub) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4372](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4372)
-   Adding log pointer to stac class instantiation to connect to reader logs by [@​kylemann16](https://redirect.github.com/kylemann16) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4353](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4353)
-   Don't attempt to execute tree location for isolated point in `filters.litree` by [@​abellgithub](https://redirect.github.com/abellgithub) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4373](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4373)
-   Fix integer overflows in pdal::Raster. by [@​abellgithub](https://redirect.github.com/abellgithub) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4376](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4376)
-   Fix sorting/iterator dereferencing by [@​abellgithub](https://redirect.github.com/abellgithub) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4387](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4387)
-   Fix H3 warnings by [@​abellgithub](https://redirect.github.com/abellgithub) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4391](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4391)
-   Fixing typo that caused Info to not fill gpstime_maximum on copc reads. by [@​kylemann16](https://redirect.github.com/kylemann16) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4404](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4404)
-   Changes for Matlab plugin to compile under macOS. by [@​prennich](https://redirect.github.com/prennich) in [https://github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4417](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/pull/4417)

### [`v2.7.2`](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/releases/tag/2.7.2)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/compare/2.7.1...2.7.2)

### Bug Fixes

-   Fix overflow in `pdal::Raster` [#​4376](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4376)
-   Warn instead of error when `readers.las` GlobalEncoding is not set correctly for WKT [#​4414](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4414)
-   `gpstime_maximum` not being correctly read in `readers.copc` [#​4404](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4404) (Thanks [@​kylemann16](https://redirect.github.com/kylemann16)!)
-   Default `writer.bpf.coord_id` to `auto` [#​4428](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4428)

### Docs

-   Fix rotation matrix example in `filters.transformation` doc (Thanks [@​ovipaul](https://redirect.github.com/ovipaul)!) [#​4394](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4394)
-   Workshop updates [#​4400](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4400)  (Thanks [@​TomTheTonk](https://redirect.github.com/TomTheTonk)!)
-   Describe `readers.las.threads` [#​4409](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4409) (Thanks [@​msmitherdc](https://redirect.github.com/msmitherdc)!)
-   Fix doc message in `filters.crop` [#​4419](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4419) (Thanks [@​pierotofy](https://redirect.github.com/pierotofy)!)
-   Properly document `option_file` [#​4425](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4425)
-   Update links for Python (Thanks [@​jomey](https://redirect.github.com/jomey)!) [#​4378](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4378)

### Build

-   Relax PROJ dependency to 9.0+ [`5c5611a`](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/commit/5c5611a8a0b7c723127789050fdb2bdeb8e2f984)
-   `STANDALONE` build config for `filters.cpd` [#​4396](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4396)

-   E57 plugins need to link against PDAL in `STANDALONE` builds [#​4421](https://redirect.github.com/PDAL/PDAL/issues/4421)

### Issue

The changes to setting the plugin path (#106) seem trigger an error warning when activating an environment. Using version 3.3.0 did not show any warnings.

The install command with warning
```
conda env create -n pdal python-pdal
```

Error message:
```
pj_obj_create: Cannot find proj.db
```

The install command with no warning
```
conda env create -n pdal python-pdal=3.3.0
```

Activate via:
```
conda activate pdal
```

### Installed packages

```shell
python-pdal
```

branch"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-16T07:09:54Z","updated_at":"2024-10-16T18:28:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/PROJ/pulls/4279","html_url":"https://github.com/OSGeo/PROJ/pull/4279","diff_url":"https://github.com/OSGeo/PROJ/pull/4279.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/OSGeo/PROJ/pull/4279.patch","merged_at":null},"body":""Trigger" for this (pun intented) is that most of the time spent while building GDAL Docker image when cross-building to arm64 is spent on building proj.db (close to 7.5h for a target Ubuntu 24.04 arm64 !). Setting this new option should cut that to a few minutes.

```

.. option:: PROJ_DB_FAST_BUILD=OFF

    .. versionadded:: 9.5.1

    By default, creation of :file:`proj.db` involves inserting consistency check
    triggers before inserting data records, to be able to catch potential
    inconsistencies. Such checks are useful for core PROJ developers when they
    update the database content, or for advanced PROJ users that customize the
    content of the database. However those checks come with a non-negligible cost.
    On modern hardware, building :file:`proj.db` with those checks enabled takes
    about 50 to 60 seconds (and on scenarios where PROJ is built for other
    architectures with full emulation, several hours). When setting this option
    to ON, those triggers are inserted after data records, which decreases the
    build time to about 3 seconds.
    In short, setting this option to ON is safe if you do not customize yourself
    the .sql files used to build :file:`proj.db`
```

Timings on my machine:

- before this PR:

```
$ time make generate_proj_db
[100%] Generating proj.db
[100%] Built target generate_proj_db

real	0m54,752s
user	0m53,968s
sys	0m0,648s

$ md5sum data/proj.db
beecdc018b4a5131229709b3c7747036  data/proj.db

$ echo ".dump" | sqlite3 data/proj.db | md5sum
64e446efdc5c18e398cc7b6b2e4b3086  -
```

- with this PR, not setting PROJ_DB_FAST_BUILD (so OFF):

Same as above

- with this PR, setting PROJ_DB_FAST_BUILD=ON

```
$ cmake .. -DPROJ_DB_FAST_BUILD=ON

$ time make generate_proj_db
[100%] Generating proj.db
[100%] Built target generate_proj_db

real	0m3,243s
user	0m2,876s
sys	0m0,204s

$ md5sum data/proj.db
1955dfdc3f7abada3890bf9b7592770a  data/proj.db

$ echo ".dump" | sqlite3 data/proj.db | md5sum
64e446efdc5c18e398cc7b6b2e4b3086  -
```

One can notice that the binary content of proj.db is not exactly the same, however the result of dumping it to SQL is exactly the same. The reason for the slight difference is that in PROJ_DB_FAST_BUILD=ON we also skip creating a fake table and trigger, which influences the "schema version number" of the SQLite3 database, which is a non significant difference.

Cf the diff of the ``od -x`` output, which shows that only a few bytes in the SQLite3 header are different.

```
$ diff -u proj.db.slow.txt proj.db.fast.txt
--- proj.db.slow.txt	2024-10-16 08:50:07.211601573 +0200 +++ proj.db.fast.txt	2024-10-16 08:50:16.155615860 +0200 @@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
 0000000 5153 694c 6574 6620 726f 616d 2074 0033
-0000020 0010 0101 4000 2020 0000 1100 0000 d208
-0000040 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 6700 0000 0400
+0000020 0010 0101 4000 2020 0000 2500 0000 d208
+0000040 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 6300 0000 0400
 0000060 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0100 0000 0000
 0000100 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000
-0000120 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 1100
+0000120 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 2500
 0000140 2e00 d93f 0005 0000 0f1a 007e 0000 d208
 0000160 fb0f f60f f10f ec0f e70f e20f dd0f d80f
 0000200 d30f ce0f c90f c40f bf0f ba0f b50f b00f
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/PROJ/issues/4279/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/PROJ/issues/4279/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/PROJ/issues/comments/2417600092","html_url":"https://github.com/OSGeo/PROJ/pull/4279#issuecomment-2417600092","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/PROJ/issues/4279","id":2417600092,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAiYwrc6QGaZc","user":{"login":"hobu","id":87751,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg3NzUx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/87751?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu","html_url":"https://github.com/hobu","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-16T18:28:02Z","updated_at":"2024-10-16T18:28:02Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Why would we add an option called `PROJ_DB_FAST_BUILD`? {"repository_id":828078200,"push_id":19599409545,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"aa5ec89fcd436c9478a1aea980181ae399c591d5","before":"e940dd8cee37e1b1e0089121161740a2406c4eb3","commits":[{"sha":"aa5ec89fcd436c9478a1aea980181ae399c591d5","author":{"email":"ca8530d180d8616a9907d4383faed940806312ef@gmail.com","name":"Xylar Asay-Davis"},"message":"Enable ppc64le (#9)

* Enable ppc64le

This should be possible now that support for ppc64le has been
re-enabled for libgdal
https://github.com/conda-forge/gdal-feedstock/pull/968

* MNT: Re-rendered with conda-build 24.5.1, conda-smithy 3.37.1, and conda-forge-pinning 2024.07.31.04.56.44

* strip architectures from flags for native builds

* don't depend on leading whitespace in sed-replacement

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [ ] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [ ] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [ ] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [ ] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [ ] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

I appreciate the fun easter egg, but it does bring up the topic of PROJ maintaining authorities. I thought project policy was to be not to do so given we have no way to upstream them to regularized standards bodies in a way that doesn't break their definition(s). 

I understand the joke coming from @jjimenezshaw's talk, but I admit I'm a bit concerned that PROJ would be adding authorities. I see the PR is hiding it by masking it in the SQL queries, but TBH, I don't know if that's actually better. I'm not 👎 against the PR, but I think we as a project should state our stance on maintaining alternative definition authorities.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/OSGeo/PROJ/issues/comments/2211770117/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

manipulation","user":{"login":"hobu","id":87751,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg3NzUx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/87751?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu","html_url":"https://github.com/hobu","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hobu/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"<!--
Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

+    - cmake
+    - ninja
+  host:

+    - libabseil
+
+test:
+  commands:

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
